title: Tuple Unpacking Practice
description: Create a tuple called **colors** with the values `'red'`, `'green'`, `'blue'` (in that order). Then **unpack** it into three variables and print each variable on its own line.
starter_code: # Create your tuple and unpack it here

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'colors' that holds a tuple with the items 'red', 'green', 'blue'
code_requirements.fail_message: Make sure you create a tuple variable called 'colors' with three string items: 'red', 'green', 'blue'.

code_requirements.description: Unpacks the tuple into three separate variables
code_requirements.fail_message: Use tuple unpacking to assign the three items to three variables: var1, var2, var3 = colors

hints: Create the tuple with parentheses: colors = ('red', 'green', 'blue')
Unpack with: c1, c2, c3 = colors
Print each variable on its own line: print(c1), print(c2), print(c3)
